questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall the order of electromagnetic waves
questions.question: Which electromagnetic wave has the highest frequency?
questions.correctAnswer: Gamma rays
questions.explanation: Gamma rays have the highest frequency and shortest wavelength in the electromagnetic spectrum.
questions.senNote: Gamma rays have the highest frequency and shortest wavelength in the electromagnetic spectrum.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe refraction into a denser medium
questions.question: Light passes from air into glass. Which change happens to the light?
options: It speeds up and bends away from the normal.
It keeps the same speed and does not change direction.
It slows down and bends away from the normal.
It slows down and bends towards the normal.
questions.correctAnswer: It slows down and bends towards the normal.
questions.explanation: Light slows down in glass, so it bends towards the normal.
questions.senNote: Light slows down in glass, so it bends towards the normal.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe refraction into a denser medium

options: It keeps the same speed and does not change direction.
It slows down and bends away from the normal.
It slows down and bends towards the normal.
It speeds up and bends away from the normal.
questions.correctAnswer: It slows down and bends towards the normal.
questions.explanation: Light slows down in glass, so it bends towards the normal.
questions.senNote: Light slows down in glass, so it bends towards the normal.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Link frequency to pitch
questions.question: Which change makes a sound higher in pitch?
options: Increasing the amplitude of the wave
Increasing the frequency of the wave
questions.correctAnswer: Increasing the frequency of the wave
questions.explanation: Pitch depends on frequency. Higher frequency means higher pitch.
questions.senNote: Pitch depends on frequency. Higher frequency means higher pitch.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain one use of ultrasound
questions.question: Why is ultrasound useful for scanning inside the human body?
options: It is ionising so it passes through all tissue.
It travels only through a vacuum.
It always carries more energy than X-rays.
It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
questions.correctAnswer: It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
questions.explanation: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries between tissues and is safe because it is non-ionising.
questions.senNote: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries between tissues and is safe because it is non-ionising.

It always carries more energy than X-rays.
questions.correctAnswer: It can be reflected to make images and it is non-ionising.
questions.explanation: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries between tissues and is safe because it is non-ionising.
questions.senNote: Ultrasound reflects at boundaries between tissues and is safe because it is non-ionising.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Use wave speed = frequency x wavelength
questions.question: A wave has a frequency of 15 Hz and a wavelength of 0.6 m. What is the wave speed?
